purpose: URL inventory of candidate Hawaiian-language and Hawaiian-parallel sources, grouped by the installed collection tool best suited to a first-pass pull. This is a routing config, not a license determination. No bulk data is fetched here. Rights status is a hint; per-source legal review is required before any publication.

rights_status_legend.public_domain_candidate: Source itself appears to be in the public domain or pre-1925 US works; still requires per-document confirmation.
rights_status_legend.open_license_candidate: Source advertises an open license (e.g., CC BY-SA 4.0, CC BY 2.0 FR, Apache-2.0); flow-down conditions must be respected.
rights_status_legend.rights_review_required: Mixed or unclear rights; manual per-document review before any use beyond evaluation.
rights_status_legend.eval_only: Use restricted to evaluation/benchmark dev/test; never train on this source.
rights_status_legend.unknown_review_required: Rights posture not yet established; treat as restricted until reviewed.

requests_tenacity.tool_description: Direct HTTP GET with retries/backoff (requests + tenacity). Use for small, stable, well-known endpoints: dump indexes, single PDFs, well-formed JSON/TSV files, and per-page polite fetches with manual rate limiting.

sources.name: Hawaiian Wikipedia — XML dump (latest)
sources.license_observed: CC BY-SA 4.0 + GFDL (per-revision; see Wikimedia Terms)
sources.collection_notes: Cleanest licensing posture per data-pipeline.md §Stage 1. Pull dump, then extract with a WikiExtractor-style pass downstream. Capture the dump-status JSON alongside the XML for reproducibility.

sources.name: Hawaiian Wikipedia — dump status manifest
sources.license_observed: Wikimedia infrastructure metadata
sources.collection_notes: Use to pin SHA1s of the actual dump files before downloading.

sources.name: Hawaiian Wikipedia — interlanguage link API (haw ↔ en)
sources.license_observed: CC BY-SA 4.0
sources.collection_notes: Used to enumerate haw↔en comparable doc pairs per data-pipeline.md §Stage 2. Respect MediaWiki API etiquette: maxlag=5, identify User-Agent, low concurrency.

sources.name: Hawaiian Wiktionary — dump
sources.license_observed: CC BY-SA 4.0
sources.collection_notes: Small but useful for lexicon / tokenizer audit fodder (ʻokina, kahakō coverage).

sources.name: FLORES-200 — devtest archive (Hugging Face mirror)
sources.license_observed: CC BY-SA 4.0 (FLORES-200) — but project policy is eval-only
sources.collection_notes: Per data-pipeline.md §Stage 2: hash all FLORES sentences into eval_hashes.jsonl BEFORE any other ingest. Never train on FLORES. Confirm hawn_Latn presence in the pinned release; if absent, downgrade priority and document.

sources.name: Tatoeba — Hawaiian sentences and links (per-language exports)
sources.license_observed: CC BY 2.0 FR (per Tatoeba site)
sources.collection_notes: Hash all pairs before deciding train vs dev (data-pipeline.md §Stage 2). Small volume; respect attribution flow-down.

sources.name: OPUS — haw-containing parallel subsets (Tatoeba, QED, Ubuntu, GNOME, KDE)
sources.license_observed: Per-corpus (varies). JW300 explicitly excluded per data-pipeline.md unless ToS re-verified.
sources.collection_notes: Software-l10n sets are domain-skewed; tag register=software-l10n and cap. Confirm exact version IDs at fetch time.

sources.name: NLLB Seed / NLLB-MD (Hawaiian slices, if present)
sources.license_observed: Per dataset card; mined data is noisy and rights-mixed
sources.collection_notes: NLLB-mined is referenced as a `source` value in data-pipeline.md §Stage 2 schema. Confirm hawn_Latn coverage and license before pulling. Treat as last-resort, not P0.

sources.name: Baibala Hemolele — official site (verse-aligned Hawaiian Bible)
sources.license_observed: Site ToS governs; pinning a pre-1925 edition (e.g., 1868 Andrews/Bingham) is the public-domain candidate path.
sources.collection_notes: Per data-pipeline.md §Stage 2: pin one Hawaiian edition + one PD English edition (KJV/ASV). Verse-ID alignment is deterministic. Cap ≤30% of parallel-train tokens, 0% of dev/test. Snapshot ToS per fetch; polite rate (≤1 req/sec).

sources.name: English Bible — public-domain editions for verse-pair anchor (KJV / ASV)
sources.license_observed: KJV / ASV are public domain in the US; eBible packaging metadata may carry its own license — record both.
sources.collection_notes: Pair with a single pinned Hawaiian edition for verse-aligned Bible. USFM preserves verse IDs cleanly.

internetarchive.tool_description: Use the `internetarchive` Python client for archive.org item-level metadata and file downloads. Best when collection IDs are known and the item already lives on archive.org with stable file lists.

sources.name: archive.org — Ulukau / Hawaiian newspaper (nūpepa) mirrored items
sources.license_observed: Per-item; many pre-1925 nūpepa are public_domain_candidate by US copyright term, but per-item review required. Ulukau-mirrored items may carry additional terms.
sources.collection_notes: Per data-pipeline.md §52–53 and §214: pre-1925 nūpepa kept private (prototype_only=true); not for bulk publication. Use `ia` CLI / library to enumerate items by collection and language facet, capture metadata.json + files.xml + the per-item ToS snapshot. Manual review of ~50 random docs before bulk ingest.

sources.name: archive.org — Hawaiian-language books / readers / dictionaries
sources.license_observed: Per-item; pre-1925 imprints are public_domain_candidate.
sources.collection_notes: Useful for Pukui-Elbert / Andrews dictionary lookups and Hawaiian readers. Narrow register — flag accordingly.

sources.name: archive.org — Baibala Hemolele scanned editions (pre-1925)
sources.license_observed: Pre-1925 imprints are public_domain_candidate in the US; confirm imprint year per item.
sources.collection_notes: An alternative / supplement to baibala.org, useful for pinning a specific historical edition (e.g., 1868 Andrews/Bingham) named in data-pipeline.md.

wayback_cdx_toolkit.tool_description: Use `wayback` and `cdx_toolkit` to query the Wayback Machine CDX index for historical snapshots, then fetch specific captures. Best when the live site is gone, rate-limited, or when we need a stable point-in-time snapshot for provenance.

sources.name: Wayback CDX — Ulukau snapshots (nūpepa portal + per-issue pages)
sources.license_observed: Wayback Machine ToS; underlying content rights are Ulukau's.
sources.collection_notes: Use Wayback when ulukau.org is unreachable or rate-limited. Always record both the Wayback capture URL and the original_url + timestamp.

sources.name: Wayback CDX — nupepa.org legacy snapshots
sources.license_observed: Per-snapshot underlying rights apply.
sources.collection_notes: Older nūpepa portal mirrors that may have moved or shut down. Wayback gives a stable historical handle.

sources.name: Wayback CDX — OHA / DOE Kaiapuni / UH Hawaiian-language pages
sources.license_observed: Government / educational rights are mixed; per-doc review per data-pipeline.md §Stage 1 / §Stage 2.
sources.collection_notes: For bilingual PDFs (OHA reports, DOE Kaiapuni curriculum, UH publications). Use CDX to enumerate, but pull each PDF only after rights review.

scrapy_scrapy_warc.tool_description: Use Scrapy + scrapy-warc for polite, structured crawls of multi-page sites where bulk pulls need WARC provenance. Required for nūpepa-style enumeration that spans thousands of pages.

sources.name: Ulukau — Hawaiian-language newspapers (nūpepa) crawl
sources.license_observed: Ulukau ToS governs crawl behavior; pre-1925 underlying content is public_domain_candidate. Pre-1925 bulk kept private per data-pipeline.md §53 / §214.
sources.collection_notes: Politeness is mandatory: respect robots.txt, ≤1 concurrent request, ≥1s delay, identifying User-Agent with contact, AUTOTHROTTLE_ENABLED. Capture ToS snapshot at crawl start. Write WARC via scrapy-warc to keep raw bytes. Per data-pipeline.md §214: ToS snapshot, polite fetch, OCR-confidence capture, paragraph-level LID, manual review of ~50 random docs before bulk ingest.

sources.name: Hawaiian Wikisource — public-domain Hawaiian texts
sources.license_observed: CC BY-SA 4.0 wrapper over public-domain source texts.
sources.collection_notes: Smaller than nūpepa, cleaner text per data-pipeline.md §90. Prefer the MediaWiki API with `requests_tenacity` for enumeration; only use Scrapy if a full WARC of the rendered pages is desired for provenance parity with other crawls.

sources.name: Awaiaulu — public Hawaiian translations / readers
sources.license_observed: Per-page; modern Hawaiian prose with mixed rights per data-pipeline.md §98 / §243.
sources.collection_notes: High quality, low volume. Crawl only public pages; do NOT scrape kūpuna/named-tradition-bearer materials without explicit permission. Hard-escalate categories from data-pipeline.md §52 apply.

sources.name: OHA — Office of Hawaiian Affairs publications portal
sources.language_content: haw + eng (bilingual reports / Ka Wai Ola pages)
sources.license_observed: Government-adjacent / publisher rights mixed; per-doc review.
sources.collection_notes: Side-by-side or sequential bilingual PDFs are useful for document-level parallel data per data-pipeline.md §244. Confirm reuse terms before any redistribution.

sources.name: Hawaii DOE — Kaiapuni (Hawaiian-medium) program pages
sources.license_observed: State-government materials; rights vary, fair-use posture is not assumed.
sources.collection_notes: Per data-pipeline.md §97 / §244: mixed rights, manual per-doc review. Crawl is for inventorying only — do not bulk-redistribute.

sources.name: UH Mānoa — Hawaiian-language scholarly / open repository pages
sources.license_observed: Per-item rights; some items CC-licensed, others all-rights-reserved.
sources.collection_notes: ScholarSpace exposes per-item license metadata; capture it verbatim in provenance. Useful for high-quality modern Hawaiian academic prose.

trafilatura_selectolax.tool_description: HTML main-text extraction (trafilatura) and fast DOM parsing (selectolax) for cleaning already-fetched pages. Not a fetcher — these are post-fetch extractors used downstream of any of the fetch buckets above.

sources.name: Ulukau / nūpepa rendered pages — main-text extraction pass
urls: (consumes raw HTML/WARC produced by the scrapy_scrapy_warc bucket)
sources.license_observed: inherits_from_source
sources.collection_notes: Strip boilerplate (nav, page-furniture). For OCR'd newspaper pages, trafilatura's article-detection is unreliable — fall back to selectolax with site-specific selectors and keep the raw OCR block as a parallel field.

sources.name: Hawaiian Wikisource rendered pages — main-text extraction pass
sources.license_observed: CC BY-SA 4.0 (inherited)
sources.collection_notes: Prefer parsing the dump XML directly when available; HTML extraction is the fallback path.

sources.name: OHA / DOE / UH bilingual HTML pages — extraction pass
urls: (consumes raw HTML produced by the scrapy_scrapy_warc bucket)
sources.license_observed: inherits_from_source
sources.collection_notes: Extracted text is only safe for downstream use after the per-doc rights review noted on the upstream entry.

yt_dlp.tool_description: Use yt-dlp ONLY to retrieve subtitles / closed captions from videos that explicitly permit reuse, or where we have rights confirmation. Audio/video bytes are out of scope here.

sources.name: Public Hawaiian-language video transcripts — captions only, rights-confirmed
urls: (no concrete channels listed by default — populate only after rights confirmation per channel)
sources.license_observed: Per-video license metadata only; YouTube's default ToS does NOT grant reuse.
sources.collection_notes: Per requirements.txt header ("audio/video transcripts where rights allow"): pull `--write-subs --write-auto-subs --skip-download` ONLY for items that publish a permissive license (e.g., Creative Commons on YouTube) or where we have written permission. Keep this bucket empty until a channel passes rights review with Linus.

deferred_or_excluded.name: JW300 — haw subset
deferred_or_excluded.reason: Explicitly excluded in data-pipeline.md §233 unless ToS re-verified. Do not fetch.

deferred_or_excluded.name: Hard-escalate categories (mele, oli, pule, moʻolelo from named tradition-bearers, moʻokūʻauhau)
deferred_or_excluded.reason: Excluded per data-pipeline.md §52 until a cultural-review owner exists, even for prototype use.

deferred_or_excluded.name: General web crawls / Common Crawl Hawaiian slices
deferred_or_excluded.reason: Undercurated scrapes are out of scope per team posture (Linus). Revisit only with a per-document provenance plan.
